content.speakerText: I’m so sorry—I’ve gotta cancel tonight. My dog’s sick.
content.speakerTextCn: 太抱歉了，今晚得取消，我狗生病了。
expectedAnswers: Oh no! Hope your dog feels better soon. Can we plan for next week?
That’s too bad—I hope Fido’s okay. Let’s reschedule when you’re free!
No worries, health comes first. Text me when you’re up for it.
evaluationCriteria: 是否表达了理解和关心（如 hope...okay / feels better）
是否提出改期或开放性安排（如 reschedule / next week / text me）
是否避免指责或冷漠语气（如 not saying 'again?' or 'whatever'）

content.speakerText: I’ll make it up to you—how about dinner this weekend?
content.speakerTextCn: 我一定补偿你，这周末一起吃饭？
expectedAnswers: That sounds great! Maybe Italian? I’ve been missing pasta!
Yes, but let’s pick a day that works for both of us—no last-minute changes!
Definitely! I’m free Saturday night. How about that new place downtown?
evaluationCriteria: 是否明确接受补偿提议（如 yes / definitely / sounds great）
是否加入具体细节提升诚意（如菜系、日期、地点）
是否适当表达期望（如避免抱怨，但可温和提醒）

content.speakerText: I hate to cancel, but my flight got delayed and I won’t make it to the party.
content.speakerTextCn: 真不想取消，但我的航班延误了，赶不上派对了。
expectedAnswers: Oh no, that’s so frustrating! Safe travels—let’s catch up soon!
No problem—flights are unpredictable. Maybe we can do brunch next week?
That’s a bummer, but I totally get it. Let me know when you’re back in town!
evaluationCriteria: 是否表达同理心（如 frustrating / bummer / get it）
是否提供替代方案或开放性结尾（如 catch up / brunch next week）
是否使用自然口语表达（避免机械或书面语）
